solve_notes: Narrowed the catch block from `FilesystemException` to `UnableToReadFile` and added a second catch for the broader `FilesystemException` that rethrows it directly. This ensures that `PathTraversalDetected`, `CorruptedPathDetected`, and other security-critical exceptions propagate without being swallowed into `UnableToProvideChecksum`.
task_text:
Fix CalculateChecksumFromStream wrapping security exceptions in UnableToProvideChecksum

In `src/CalculateChecksumFromStream.php`, the
`calculateChecksumFromStream` trait method wraps every `FilesystemException`
thrown by `readStream` inside a new `UnableToProvideChecksum`. This
includes security-critical exceptions such as `PathTraversalDetected`
and `CorruptedPathDetected` (both implement `FilesystemException`),
which should never be reclassified as a checksum failure. Callers
and security logging systems lose the true reason for the error. Fix
the catch block to only wrap `UnableToReadFile` in
`UnableToProvideChecksum` (the expected case where the file simply
cannot be read for hashing), and re-throw any other
`FilesystemException` — especially path-security exceptions —
without wrapping.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the edited method and the exception class used in the fix. An AI agent would check PathTraversalDetected and CorruptedPathDetected to confirm they implement FilesystemException (and thus are caught by the second catch), preventing it from adding individual catch clauses for each exception type.
